title: Scoping Review Protocol in Qualitative Public Health Research in Vietnam
author: Hung Viet Nguyen

steps.title: Qualitative research provides essential contextual evidence...
steps.instruction: Qualitative research provides essential contextual evidence for health policy and program design in low- and middle-income countries. Vietnam—a lower-middle-income country of approximately 100 million people—faces a dual epidemiological burden: ongoing communicable diseases including HIV/AIDS, tuberculosis, and antimicrobial resistance (AMR), alongside accelerating non-communicable disease (NCD) and cancer transition. The period from 2020 to 2025 encompassed the COVID-19 pandemic, accelerated digital health adoption, and growing recognition of the mental health burden. Despite expanding publication of qualitative public health research from Vietnam in internationally indexed journals, no scoping review has systematically characterized the thematic domains and methodological approaches of this literature for the 2020–2025 period. This review addresses that gap.

steps.title: This review aims to
steps.instruction: This review aims to: 1. Identify the thematic domains that characterize qualitative public health research in Vietnam from 2020 to 2025. 2. Examine the study designs, data collection methods, and analytical approaches employed in this body of literature.

steps.title: Scoping review following the five-stage framework...
steps.instruction: Scoping review following the five-stage framework of Arksey and O'Malley (2005), extended by Levac et al. (2010), and reported using PRISMA-ScR guidelines (Tricco et al., 2018). PCC Framework Population/Concept: Public health topics and health services research in Vietnam Concept: Qualitative or mixed-methods research design and methodology Context: Vietnam, 2020–2025, internationally indexed journals

steps.title: Databases
steps.instruction: Databases: PubMed (MEDLINE), Europe PMC (MEDLINE + PubMed Central), Scopus Search date: January 2025 Language: English Keyword Groups (combined with Boolean AND) Group 1 — Geographic scope (Context): "Vietnam"[MeSH Terms] OR "Vietnam"[Title/Abstract] Group 2 — Qualitative methodology (Concept): "qualitative research"[MeSH] OR "qualitative study"[tiab] OR "in-depthinterview"[tiab] OR "focus group"[tiab] OR "thematicanalysis"[tiab] OR "phenomenolog*"[tiab] OR "mixedmethod*"[tiab] OR "key informant"[tiab] OR "groundedtheory"[tiab] OR "ethnograph*"[tiab] Group 3 — Public health domain (Population/Concept): "public health"[MeSH] OR "health system*"[tiab] OR "healthbehav*"[tiab] OR "health polic*"[tiab] OR "healthservice*"[tiab] OR "health promot*"[tiab] MeSH = Medical SubjectHeadings (NLM); tiab = title/abstract field tag (PubMed). Equivalent syntaxapplied in Europe PMC (TITLE_ABS field; SRC:MED OR SRC:PMC) and Scopus(TITLE-ABS-KEY field; DOCTYPE(ar); LANGUAGE(english)). Asterisk (*) =truncation operator.

steps.title: A B C 1 Criterion Inclusion...
steps.instruction: A B C 1 Criterion Inclusion Exclusion 2 C1 — Indexing Published in MEDLINE- or Scopus-indexed journal Non-indexed journals 3 C2 — Design Qualitative or mixed-methods design explicitly described in abstract Quantitative-only studies 4 C3 — Setting Vietnam as primary study site; multi-country only if Vietnam-specific findings independently extractable Multi-country without extractable Vietnam findings; Vietnamese diaspora outside Vietnam 5 Additional English language; peer-reviewed article; 2020–2025; abstract available; public health focus Editorials, letters, conference abstracts; outside date range; no abstract; non-public health focus

steps.title: Records from all three databases were...
steps.instruction: Records from all three databases were imported into a structured spreadsheet and deduplicated using PMID and DOI matching (110 duplicates removed: 31 PubMed–Europe PMC; 79 Scopus–PubMed/Europe PMC). Eight Europe PMC records were excluded at the initial scan. The remaining 265 records underwent title/abstract screening, applying Criteria 2 and 3. Records meeting eligibility at the title/abstract level proceeded to full-text review. Full-text exclusions were documented with reasons. Screening and full-text review were conducted by the first author. A random 10% sample (n=12) was independently verified for consistency.

steps.title: Bibliographic details
steps.instruction: Bibliographic details: first author, year, journal, DOI

steps.title: Study design
steps.instruction: Study design: qualitative, mixed-methods, or ethnographic

steps.title: Primary data collection method(s)
steps.instruction: Primary data collection method(s): IDI, FGD, ethnographic observation, or combination

steps.title: Analytical approach
steps.instruction: Analytical approach: thematic analysis, content/framework analysis, or phenomenological

steps.title: Geographic setting
steps.instruction: Geographic setting: urban or rural/highland

steps.title: Sampling strategy
steps.instruction: Sampling strategy: purposive or snowball/respondent-driven

steps.title: Methodological quality was appraised using the...
steps.instruction: Methodological quality was appraised using the CASP Qualitative Checklist (10 domains). Reporting completeness was assessed against the 32-item COREQ checklist. Quality appraisal informed interpretation of findings but was not used as an exclusion criterion, consistent with scoping review methodology (Levac et al., 2010).

steps.title: A narrative synthesis was conducted
steps.instruction: A narrative synthesis was conducted. Studies were organized into ten thematic clusters based on their primary public health focus, determined inductively during data extraction through constant comparison of study objectives, MeSH terms, and abstract content. Labels were iteratively grouped until each cluster demonstrated internal coherence and meaningful differentiation from adjacent clusters; studies resisting clear primary classification were assigned to an "Other cross-cutting topics" cluster. All assignments were cross-validated against each study's MeSH terms and author-stated objectives. Methodological characteristics were summarized descriptively using counts and proportions.

steps.instruction: 3 After deduplication & screening 265 screened; 181 full-text assessed 4 Included studies 116 studies 5 Publication period 2020–2025; 57% (n=

steps.instruction: published in 2023–2025 6 Thematic clusters 10 clusters (determined inductively) 7 Largest clusters NCD & Cancer (n=22), MCH (n=19), Health Systems & Policy (n=

steps.title: 8 Primary data collection In-depth interviews...
steps.instruction: 8 Primary data collection In-depth interviews — IDI (n=115; 99%) 9 FGD use Focus group discussions (n=59; 51%), mostly combined with IDI 10 Mixed-methods designs 26 studies (22%) 11 Analytical approach Thematic analysis (n=107; 92%) 12 Theoretical framework 1 study (1%) — critical gap 13 Reflexivity reported 14 studies (12%) — significantly underreported 14 COREQ full adherence 16 studies (14%); partial in 86% 15 Urban setting 91 studies (78%) 16 Rural/highland setting 25 studies (22%)

steps.title: Strengths include HIV/AIDS care and stigma,...
steps.instruction: Strengths include HIV/AIDS care and stigma, COVID-19 community response, and health systems research. Three critical methodological gaps characterize the corpus:

steps.title: Theoretical framework application is critically rare...
steps.instruction: Theoretical framework application is critically rare (1%) — findings remain largely descriptiverather than explanatory

steps.title: Reflexivity reporting is systematically inadequate (12%)—a...
steps.instruction: Reflexivity reporting is systematically inadequate (12%)—a substantive validity concern givenresearcher positionality effects in Vietnamese research contexts

steps.title: Geographic concentration in urban settings (78%)...
steps.instruction: Geographic concentration in urban settings (78%) underrepresents rural and highland populations with the highest unmet health needs. Targeted capacity development addressing these gaps will strengthen Vietnam's qualitative evidence base and its contribution to global public health knowledge.

steps.title: Embase, CINAHL, and PsycINFO were not searched
steps.instruction: Embase, CINAHL, and PsycINFO were not searched. Only English-language publications were included.Vietnamese-language research in domestic journals represents a systematic gap. Data extraction was performed by a single author (10% independent verification). No formal protocol registration was completed prior to the review.
